tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and take three deep breaths, visualizing God's authority as a protective, guiding system in your community and personal life

two_step.reflect_question: How can I demonstrate respect for authority while maintaining personal integrity?

options.response: Create a weekly personal checklist of civic responsibilities
Develop a prayer journal specifically for government leaders
Attend local town halls or community meetings

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a shield representing how God's authority protects and guides your community

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of authority and submission changed after reading Romans 13?
options: I see authority more as a God-ordained system
I recognize my role in supporting constructive governance
I understand submission doesn't mean blind obedience
I feel more responsible for being a positive community member

three_choice.question: Which theological perspective on governmental authority resonates most deeply?

choices.expansion: Authorities exist within a sovereign theological framework, suggesting cosmic order beyond human political structures.
Personal conduct determines one's relationship with both civic and spiritual authorities.
Yielding to earthly authorities becomes an act of theological humility and spiritual discipline.

socratic_nudge.question: If divine authority ultimately transcends human governance, at what point does principled resistance become a spiritual imperative?

moral_slider.question: How do individual conscience and governmental authority balance in faithful living?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of civic participation evolved?
options: Understand submission as a form of spiritual discipline
See political engagement as a moral responsibility
Appreciate complexity of divine and human authority

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Authority and Governance
thematic_summaries.summary: Paul teaches that governmental authorities are divinely ordained instruments of God's order and justice. All legitimate governmental power ultimately derives from God's sovereignty, and Christians are called to respectful submission.

thematic_summaries.title: Christian Civic Responsibility
thematic_summaries.summary: Believers are instructed to be model citizens who respect civil law and contribute positively to societal order. Submission to authority is presented as a spiritual discipline that reflects God's design for human community.

reevaluation.question: How should Christians interpret divine authority in contexts of unjust or oppressive governance?
options: Absolute submission regardless of moral considerations
Principled non-violent resistance following biblical ethics
Active advocacy for systemic reform through legal channels
Prioritizing spiritual kingdom principles over earthly structures
